title: Archived -  Suppliers of technical-grade ethanol for use in the production of hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants - Canada.ca
content:
## Archived - Suppliers of technical-grade ethanol for use in the production of hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants
Heath Canada introduced an interim measure that permits the use of certain authorized sources of technical-grade ethanol. We took this step to meet the urgent health need for hand sanitizers and disinfectants during the COVID-19 pandemic. We are discontinuing the interim measure as the supply of ethanol has stabilized and the demand for technical-grade ethanol has decreased. Suppliers and distributors are expected to stop producing, reselling and/or distributing technical-grade ethanol. Manufacturers are to return to using approved grades of ethanol for manufacturing after the authorized dates to produce, resell/distribute or sell.
Health Canada granted temporary authorization to the companies listed below to supply technical-grade ethanol for the production of hand sanitizers and/or hard-surface disinfectants. To be given temporary authorization, these companies required an assessment and authorization by Health Canada.
Authorized suppliers of technical-grade ethanol are not distributors of technical-grade ethanol or manufacturers of hand sanitizers or hard-surface disinfectants containing technical-grade ethanol .
Technical-grade ethanol obtained from authorized suppliers cannot be blended when producing hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants. Only authorized distributors may further sell technical-grade ethanol obtained from authorized suppliers.
The risk assessment summary report contains the information we used to allow and discontinue the temporary use of technical-grade ethanol in hand sanitizers and hard-surface disinfectants.
Companies not on this list may face compliance and enforcement action if they supply technical-grade ethanol for the production of hand sanitizer and/or hand-surface disinfectants without authorization. You can find information about non-compliant products in the recall advisory .

Return to footnote * referrer
Note 1: Permolex Ltd. was an approved technical-grade ethanol producer between May 1 and June 8, 2020. Since June 8, 2020, Permolex Ltd. is recognized as a producer of Food Chemicals Codex (FCC)-grade ethanol. Hand sanitizers manufactured with ethanol produced by Permolex Ltd. before June 8, 2020, must observe risk statements for technical-grade ethanol, in addition to labelling requirements set out by the antiseptic skin cleanser monograph . Hand sanitizers manufactured with ethanol produced by Permolex Ltd. on or after June 8, 2020, don't require additional risk statements for technical-grade ethanol.
Note 2: Hand sanitizers made with technical-grade ethanol containing acetaldehyde levels equal to or below 75 ppm don't require technical-grade ethanol risk statements on their labels.
Note 3: Hand sanitizers or hard-surface disinfectants containing technical-grade ethanol with higher levels of impurities (acetaldehyde levels greater than 75ppm) can be identified by reading their product labels. The labels will include the following information:
- under Medicinal Ingredients: "Ethanol XX% (technical-grade)"
- under Directions: "Adults only"
- under Warnings: "Do not use on broken or damaged skin" "Do not use if you are pregnant or breastfeeding" or "Not recommended if you are pregnant or breastfeeding" "Do not inhale"
- "Do not use on broken or damaged skin"
- "Do not use if you are pregnant or breastfeeding" or "Not recommended if you are pregnant or breastfeeding"
